objective: Create the canonical end-to-end Event Storming record for tool onboarding, from discovery of a Tool candidate through isolated evaluation and ToolGrade publication to eligibility for use in an Execution.
invariants: Tool Lab owns isolated evaluation and ToolGrade publication.
Orchestration consumes only the published ToolGrade contract and never reads Tool Lab internal models.
A ToolVersion cannot become approved without license review and at least one successful EvaluationRun.
A discovered Tool candidate cannot become automatically approved or eligible for an Execution.
Every deterministic EvaluationSuite hard gate must pass before ToolGraded(approved) is published.
Automatic approval creates no AgentBinding, CapabilityGrant, or Execution.
Each evaluation case runs in a separate clean ToolLabSession and teardown must be verified.
Deprecated ToolVersions cannot start new Executions; Agent Studio disables their bindings.
decisions: The product owner explicitly selected issue #13 as the next child of SPEC-P0-domain-discovery.

Issue #13 is owned by the full Orchestration bounded-context name for delivery while the discovery must make Tool Lab, Integration, Agent Studio, Approval & Security, and Orchestration ownership boundaries explicit.
The product owner validated the conversational Event Storming model section by section on 2026-07-13.
The product owner explicitly accepted the written canonical Event Storming record on 2026-07-13 without requesting changes.
No implementation, spec-to-main merge, push, or next-issue selection is authorized.
implementation_state: Issue #13 remains open and its GitHub Project status is Review. The accepted Event Storming record is committed on local main at 4c08593 and its accepted HandoffBrief at 7aee435. Review evidence was recorded with no pending HITL, and scripts/spec-worktree finish-feature 13 returned the clean spec worktree to spec/domain-discovery and deleted feature/13-validate-tool-onboarding-domain-flow. No product code, real Tool evaluation, ToolGrade transition, push, or spec-to-main merge occurred.
